description: The markitdown library is structured as a modular conversion pipeline that transforms various document formats into LLM-friendly Markdown. The architecture follows a Facade and Strategy pattern, where a central orchestrator (MarkItDown) manages a registry of specialized converters. Data flows from external interfaces (CLI, API, or MCP) through the core registry, which routes the input to the most appropriate converter—ranging from standard text parsers to AI-powered OCR services and cloud-based document intelligence adapters—finally producing a unified DocumentConverterResult.

components.name: Orchestration & Interface
components.description: Acts as the primary entry point for users and external systems. It manages the high-level conversion lifecycle, handles CLI commands, and exposes the library via the Model Context Protocol (MCP). It is responsible for initializing the system, detecting file types via Magika, and loading plugins.

components.name: Conversion Orchestrator & API
components.description: This component acts as the primary engine and public interface for the library. It manages the conversion flow by wrapping inputs into StreamInfo objects, detecting MIME types, and iterating through registered converters to find the most suitable handler. It provides the high-level convert methods used by developers and the CLI.

components.name: Registry & Plugin Manager
components.description: Responsible for the "Setup" phase, this component handles the instantiation of the MarkItDown object, the registration of built-in converters (e.g., PDF, Docx, Audio), and the dynamic discovery of external plugins. It maintains the internal mapping between file types and their corresponding converter implementations.

components.name: MCP Server Interface
components.description: A specialized adapter that exposes MarkItDown's capabilities via the Model Context Protocol (MCP). It allows LLMs and external agents to interact with the library as a service, handling network-specific concerns like Server-Sent Events (SSE) and HTTP streaming while delegating the actual conversion to the core orchestrator.

components_relations.relation: Populates the internal converter registry that the orchestrator queries during the conversion process.
Forwards conversion requests received via the MCP protocol to the core MarkItDown instance for processing.
Queries the registry to identify and retrieve the appropriate DocumentConverter for a given file type.

components.name: Core Conversion Logic
components.description: The central hub of the library. It maintains the registry of available converters and defines the standard data model for all conversion outputs. It routes incoming data to the most appropriate converter based on file type and priority, ensuring a consistent output format regardless of the source.

components.name: Core Abstractions & Models
components.description: Defines the foundational contracts and data structures that ensure interoperability across the library. It establishes the DocumentConverter interface that all specialized handlers must implement and the DocumentConverterResult which standardizes the output format.

components.name: Registry & Plugin Manager
components.description: Manages the lifecycle, discovery, and prioritization of converters. It provides the mechanism for internal registration and dynamically extends the library's capabilities by loading third-party converters via Python entry points.

components.name: Conversion Orchestrator
components.description: The primary routing engine that provides the public API for conversion. It normalizes various input types (URIs, Responses, Streams) and executes the internal loop that matches processed streams against registered converters based on priority and acceptance logic.

components.name: Stream & Type Analyzer
components.description: Handles low-level input normalization and content identification. It aggregates metadata from file extensions and headers, and performs deep inspection using magika to provide high-confidence type guesses used for converter selection.

components_relations.relation: Requests content type inference and metadata extraction for raw input streams.
Queries the registry to find the highest-priority converter that accepts the analyzed stream type.
Executes the conversion via the DocumentConverter interface and returns a DocumentConverterResult.
Stores and manages objects that implement the DocumentConverter base class.

components.name: Specialized Document Parsers
components.description: Implements deep parsing logic for complex document formats like PDF, Word, Excel, and PowerPoint. It includes specialized sub-systems for handling mathematical notation (converting OMML to LaTeX) and structured data extraction from tables and charts.

components.name: Word & Math Processing Engine
components.description: Implements the logic for converting Office Math Markup Language (OMML) into LaTeX and manages the pre-processing of Word documents to ensure equations are correctly formatted before final conversion.

components.name: PDF Layout & Table Parser
components.description: Specializes in extracting structured content from PDF files using heuristic-based analysis of word coordinates to reconstruct tables and maintain document flow.

components.name: Presentation & Multimedia Parser
components.description: Extracts content from PowerPoint presentations, including slide text, charts, and tables, and integrates with LLM-based captioning for image descriptions.

components.name: Specialized Format Orchestrators
components.description: Provides the primary entry points and lifecycle management for complex converters like Docx, Xlsx, and Azure Document Intelligence, delegating specific parsing tasks to internal engines.

components_relations.relation: The DocxConverter invokes pre_process_docx to identify and transform OMML math blocks into LaTeX before the standard document conversion proceeds.
The pre_process_docx function acts as a coordinator that utilizes the oMath2Latex engine to perform the recursive XML-to-string translation for mathematical notations.
The PptxConverter utilizes the llm_caption utility to generate descriptive text for visual elements (images) encountered during slide traversal.
calls
calls

components.name: AI & Vision Services
components.description: Provides enhanced conversion capabilities using AI and Computer Vision. It implements OCR-aware converters that can extract text and structure from images and scanned documents using LLM vision models, often replacing standard parsers when high-fidelity visual extraction is required.

components.name: OCR Core & PDF Integration
components.description: This component serves as the foundational engine for the subsystem. It provides the central LLMVisionOCRService, which encapsulates the logic for interacting with vision-capable LLMs. It also includes the PdfConverterWithOCR, the primary implementation for processing scanned PDF documents, and the plugin registration logic that integrates all OCR-aware converters into the MarkItDown framework.

components.name: Office OCR Extensions
components.description: This component provides specialized converters for Microsoft Office formats (Word, Excel, and PowerPoint). These converters implement format-specific logic to identify and extract visual assets—such as embedded images, charts, and diagrams—and delegate the visual analysis to the central OCR service to enrich the final Markdown output with descriptions of these elements.

registers

components.name: External & Plugin Extensions
components.description: Manages integrations with external cloud services and provides the infrastructure for third-party plugins. This allows the library to be extended with new formats (like RTF) and high-fidelity cloud-based parsing via services like Azure AI Document Intelligence.

components.name: Plugin Discovery & Infrastructure
components.description: Manages the lifecycle of external extensions by scanning Python entry points and registering third-party converters into the main registry.

components.name: Cloud Service Adapters
components.description: Provides high-fidelity document parsing by interfacing with external cloud APIs, specifically Azure AI Document Intelligence, for complex layout analysis and OCR.

components.name: OCR Extension Suite
components.description: A specialized plugin implementation that enhances standard converters with OCR capabilities using LLM vision models to extract text from images and complex documents.

components.name: MCP Server Interface
components.description: Implements the Model Context Protocol (MCP) to expose MarkItDown as a standardized tool for LLMs, supporting both local (STDIO) and remote (HTTP/SSE) execution.

components_relations.relation: Discovers and registers the OCR-enhanced converters into the core registry during initialization.
Relies on the plugin-aware core library to ensure all registered extensions (like OCR or custom plugins) are available for LLM tool calls.
Provides an alternative high-fidelity extraction path when local LLM-vision OCR is preferred over cloud-based Azure services.

Dispatches conversion requests and manages the converter registry.
Routes standard document formats (PDF, DOCX, XLSX) to specialized parsing engines.
Routes requests to OCR-enhanced converters when AI features are enabled.
Delegates conversion to cloud services or third-party plugin implementations.
